acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human output includes applied + durable watermarks grouped by namespace
- [ ] Output order is deterministic (namespace, then origin)
- [ ] Unit test covers the new formatting
description:
**Problem**
Admin status human output omits applied/durable watermarks, which are core realtime visibility signals when diagnosing lag. The data exists in API but is hidden in CLI.

**Design**
Extend render_admin_status in src/cli/render.rs to print applied/durable watermarks per namespace/origin in a deterministic order. Keep formatting concise (namespace -> origin -> seq, head state optional). Add a focused unit test for the new formatting.

**Files**
- src/cli/render.rs
title: Show applied/durable watermarks in admin status CLI output

description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/**` and `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/wal/**` are not assembly behavior tests. They exercise daemon/daemon-core protocol state machines, WAL indexing, fsck, range reads, idempotency, and receipt semantics while depending on a large pile of lower-level fixtures under `tests/integration/fixtures/{repl_*,wal*,mutation,receipt}.rs`.

**Why It Matters**
These tests make `beads-rs` look like it owns replication protocol and WAL behavior when those concepts belong to daemon crates. They also keep lower-level fixture code trapped under the assembly package.

**Design**
- Move the repl/WAL suites to owner-crate tests, most likely `crates/beads-daemon/tests/**` and `crates/beads-daemon/tests/support/**`; use `beads-daemon-core` only if a subset is purely frame/proto ownership.
- Move or rewrite the lower-level fixtures they need: `repl_frames`, `repl_peer`, `repl_transport`, `wal`, `wal_corrupt`, `mutation`, `receipt`, and any supporting fixture modules required by those tests.
- Remove the moved `repl/**` and `wal/**` declarations from `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration.rs`.

**Files To Study**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/**`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/wal/**`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/{repl_frames,repl_peer,repl_transport,wal,wal_corrupt,mutation,receipt}.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/tests/layout_runtime_config.rs`

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Repl/WAL suites no longer live under `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/**`
- [ ] Lower-level repl/WAL fixtures move with the owner tests
- [ ] `beads-rs` integration suite stops declaring the moved repl/WAL modules
- [ ] relevant owner-crate tests pass
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration` still passes after the move
title: Move repl and WAL integration suites into daemon-owned tests
